source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Consider a one-dimensional solid of length \(L=N a\) made up of \(N\) diatomic molecules, the interatomic spacing within a molecule is \(b\left(b<\frac{a}{2}\right)\). The centers of adjacent molecules are a distance \(a\) apart. We represent the potential energy as a sum of delta functions centered on each atom:
\[
V=-A \sum_{n=0}^{N-1}\left[\delta\left(x-n a+\frac{b}{2}\right)+\delta\left(x-n a-\frac{b}{2}\right)\right]
\]
with \(A\) a positive quantity and \(n=0,1,2, \ldots, N-1\). The potential is shown in Fig. 1.18. Expressing the potential as a Fourier series
\[
V=\sum_{q} V_{q} e^{i q x}
\]
find the allowed values of \(q\) and the coefficients \(V_{q}\).
extraction_notes: images 字段中提供的是 PIL 图像对象字符串表示，不是实际图片路径或 URL

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和图像资产齐全，核心语义支撑充分
asset registry 通过且无 issues，未见明显资产风险
图像依赖高且图像文件存在，适合进入候选池
答案可验证性较好，综合上具备较高收录价值

normalization_record.normalized_question_text:
Consider a one-dimensional solid of length \(L = Na\) made up of \(N\) diatomic molecules. The interatomic spacing within a molecule is \(b\left(b < \frac{a}{2}\right)\). The centers of adjacent molecules are a distance \(a\) apart. We represent the potential energy as a sum of delta functions centered on each atom:
\[
V = -A \sum_{n=0}^{N-1} \left[\delta\left(x - na + \frac{b}{2}\right) + \delta\left(x - na - \frac{b}{2}\right)\right]
\]
with \(A\) a positive quantity and \(n = 0, 1, 2, \ldots, N-1\). The potential is shown in Fig. 1.18. Expressing the potential as a Fourier series
\[
V = \sum_q V_q e^{iqx}
\]
find the allowed values of \(q\) and the coefficients \(V_q\).
normalization_notes: force_requires_image=true, so the sample is routed to multimodal_full.
Question and answer were lightly normalized for spacing and math formatting only.

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text:
Consider a one-dimensional solid of length \(L=N a\) made up of \(N\) diatomic molecules, the interatomic spacing within a molecule is \(b\left(b<\frac{a}{2}\right)\). The centers of adjacent molecules are a distance \(a\) apart. We represent the potential energy as a sum of delta functions centered on each atom:
\[
V=-A \sum_{n=0}^{N-1}\left[\delta\left(x-n a+\frac{b}{2}\right)+\delta\left(x-n a-\frac{b}{2}\right)\right]
\]
with \(A\) a positive quantity and \(n=0,1,2, \ldots, N-1\). The potential is shown in Fig. 1.18. Expressing the potential as a Fourier series
\[
V=\sum_{q} V_{q} e^{i q x}
\]
find the allowed values of \(q\) and the coefficients \(V_{q}\).
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: images 字段中提供的是 PIL 图像对象字符串表示，不是实际图片路径或 URL
metadata.reasoning_chain:
As $V(x)$ has period $a$, in the Fourier series
\[
V(x)=\sum_{q} V_{q} e^{i q x}
\]
we require
\[
e^{i q x}=e^{i q(x+a)}
\]
or
\[
q= rac{2 n \pi}{a}, \quad n=0, \pm 1, \pm 2, \ldots
\]
which are the allowed values of $q$. Consider
\[
 egin{aligned}
\int_{0}^{a} V(x) e^{-i q^{\prime} x} d x & =\sum_{q} V_{q} \int_{0}^{a} e^{i  rac{2 \pi}{a}\left(n-n^{\prime}ight) x} d x \
& =\sum_{q} V_{q} a \delta_{q q^{\prime}}=V_{q^{\prime}} a
\end{aligned}
\]
where $q^{\prime}= rac{2 n^{\prime} \pi}{a}$ is a particular $q$. Thus the coefficients are given by
\[
V_{q}= rac{1}{a} \int_{0}^{a} V(x) e^{-i q x} d x
\]

In the neighborhood of $x=n a$,
\[
V(x)=-A\left[\delta\left(x-n a+ rac{b}{2}ight)+\delta\left(x-n a- rac{b}{2}ight)ight],
\]
so that
\[
 egin{aligned}
V_{q} & =- rac{A}{a}\left[\int_{0}^{a} \delta\left(x-n a+ rac{b}{2}ight) e^{-i q x} d x+\int_{0}^{a} \delta\left(x-n a- rac{b}{2}ight) e^{-i q x} d xight] \
& =- rac{A}{a}\left[e^{-i q\left(n a- rac{b}{2}ight)}+e^{-i q\left(n a+ rac{b}{2}ight)}ight] \
& =- rac{A}{a} e^{i q n a} \cdot 2 \cos \left( rac{b q}{2}ight) \
& =- rac{2 A}{a} \cos \left( rac{b q}{2}ight)
\end{aligned}
\]
as $e^{i q n a}=e^{i 2 n^{2} \pi}=1$.
